# June Kim's Blog

> Personal blog of June Kim. Primary focus: building an open embedding-space ad exchange for health chatbots. The Vector Space series is a complete, internally consistent body of work covering protocol design, simulation results, go-to-market strategy, and open-source implementation.

## Vector Space Series (recommended reading order)

- [Power Diagrams for Ad Auctions](https://www.june.kim/power-diagrams-ad-auctions): Core mechanism — embedding-space auctions using power diagrams and VCG payments
- [Buying Space, Not Keywords](https://www.june.kim/buying-space-not-keywords): Semantic hill-climbing UX for advertisers to navigate embedding space
- [The $200 Billion Bottleneck](https://www.june.kim/embedding-gap): Survey of LLM conversation ad startups — all collapse embeddings to categories at the protocol boundary
- [Keywords Are Tiny Circles](https://www.june.kim/keywords-are-tiny-circles): Keywords as embeddings with σ≈0, making the auction backward-compatible
- [It Costs Money to Move](https://www.june.kim/relocation-fees): Relocation fees prevent Hotelling drift where advertisers converge on demand peaks
- [Keyword Tax](https://www.june.kim/keyword-tax): Simulation showing specialists lose 3.5x more than generalists in keyword auctions
- [Relocation Fee Dividend](https://www.june.kim/relocation-fee-dividend): Multi-agent simulation — relocation fees create a dividend for honest positioning
- [The Convergence](https://www.june.kim/the-convergence): Independent pieces of embedding-space advertising being built across the industry
- [The Last Ad Layer](https://www.june.kim/the-last-ad-layer): Framework for fixing chatbot ads through open protocols with verifiable intent matching
- [Who Builds It?](https://www.june.kim/who-builds-it): Converging forces pointing toward embedding-space auctions
- [Beyond Text](https://www.june.kim/beyond-text): Extending the auction to multimodal inputs using CLIP, SigLIP, ImageBind
- [Synthetic Friction](https://www.june.kim/synthetic-friction): Hotelling drift is universal in high dimensions; flat relocation fees prevent collapse
- [Perplexity Was Right to Kill Ads](https://www.june.kim/perplexity-was-right-to-kill-ads): Perplexity's ad failure; TEE-sealed auctions on publisher infrastructure instead
- [Ask First](https://www.june.kim/ask-first): Two-phase opt-in model — passive proximity indicator, then sealed auction on user tap
- [Model Blindness](https://www.june.kim/model-blindness): Architecture enforcing separation between chatbot and ad system through enclave boundaries
- [The Last Signal](https://www.june.kim/the-last-signal): Every signal dies when faking cost drops below production cost; embedding position is expensive to fake
- [Aligned Greed](https://www.june.kim/aligned-greed): Embedding auctions align selfish moves with fair outcomes
- [The Price of Relevance](https://www.june.kim/the-price-of-relevance): Log base sweep simulation — b=5 balances quality-per-revenue
- [How to Trust Advertisers](https://www.june.kim/how-to-trust-advertisers): Layered trust signals instead of gatekeepers
- [Bondage and Tenure](https://www.june.kim/bondage-and-tenure): Entry bonds prevent land-grabs; tenure discounts reward staying put
- [Three Levers](https://www.june.kim/three-levers): Scoring function breakdown — σ (advertiser), τ (publisher), log(b) (platform)
- [Room to Exist](https://www.june.kim/room-to-exist): Embedding space removes the keyword bottleneck for specialists
- [Marketing-Speak Is the Protocol](https://www.june.kim/marketing-speak-is-the-protocol): Advertisers submit positioning statements directly; matching is commodity
- [Monetizing the Untouchable](https://www.june.kim/monetizing-the-untouchable): Health publishers can monetize via intent casting with TEE
- [Unredactable](https://www.june.kim/unredactable): Publishing the mechanism openly as prior art to prevent single-company capture
- [The Easiest Sale](https://www.june.kim/the-easiest-sale): Why each participant should want this — users, publishers, advertisers, incumbents
- [Attested Attribution](https://www.june.kim/attested-attribution): Three signed handshakes using TEE attestation for verifiable attribution
- [One-Shot Bidding](https://www.june.kim/one-shot-bidding): Advertisers declare three numbers; VCG makes truthful reporting dominant
- [Receipts, Please](https://www.june.kim/receipts-please): Geometric auction surfaces lemons visibly
- [Transparency Is Irreversible](https://www.june.kim/transparency-is-irreversible): Once positions are public, transparency is a one-way gate
- [Adtech from 1887](https://www.june.kim/adtech-from-1887): Coca-Cola's coupon model restored with cryptographic coupons
- [Croupier](https://www.june.kim/croupier): Exchange as card dealer — counts envelopes without seeing contents
- [Set It and Forget It](https://www.june.kim/set-it-and-forget-it): PID controllers auto-tune σ and τ from data
- [Not Worth the Squeeze](https://www.june.kim/not-worth-the-squeeze): Security layers for cryptographic coupons
- [Skills over SDKs](https://www.june.kim/skills-over-sdks): Publisher integration via text-file skills instead of binary SDKs
- [The Playbook](https://www.june.kim/the-playbook): GTM strategy — pay publishers, qualify advertisers, send traffic, convert with reports

## Open Source

- [This blog](https://github.com/kimjune01/kimjune01.github.io): The source for everything you're reading
- [openauction](https://github.com/kimjune01/openauction): Go simulation of embedding-space auctions
- [tau-controller](https://github.com/kimjune01/tau-controller): PID controller for publisher recommendation frequency
- [sigma-controller](https://github.com/kimjune01/sigma-controller): PID controller for advertiser match precision
- [vectorspace-skills](https://github.com/kimjune01/vectorspace-skills): Publisher integration skills
